name: An Absolutely Perfect Roast Goose!
recipeIngredient: 1 10 to 12 pound goose (fresh or frozen and thawed)
recipeInstructions:
I have made a Thanksgiving goose every year for at least 15 years. I
have steadily improved my technique but finally found the perfect recipe in Cook's Magazine.
The key is using an eastern method of drying the skin, similar to Peking Duck preparation.
This results in crispy, dry, and delicious skin that everyone loves!
A frozen goose is suitable; thaw it for 24 to 48 hours before cooking (48 hours is ideal).
Prick the goose all over, especially on the breast and upper legs, using a skewer almost parallel to the bird to avoid piercing the flesh.
Fill a large pot two-thirds full of water (ensure the pot is large enough to fit the bird) and bring to a boil.
Submerge the bird neck-side down for 1 minute until goose bumps appear. Repeat with the tail side down.
Drain the goose, breast side up on a rack in a large roasting pan, and refrigerate uncovered to dry the skin for 24 to 48 hours.
On the day of cooking:
Prepare stuffing using wild rice (cooked overnight) and shitake mushrooms (soaked and chopped).
Salt and pepper the bird inside and out generously.
Preheat oven to 325°F while stuffing and sewing up the bird. Place it in a roaster on a rack breast-side up.
Roast for about 5 hours for a 12-pound goose, then let it rest undisturbed for 1.5 hours.
Check doneness by gently squeezing the upper drumstick; if it feels firm like roast beef, it's ready.
Increase heat to 400°F, transfer bird (with rack) to a jelly roll pan, and roast for another 15 minutes.
Let the goose sit uncovered for about 30 minutes before serving.
Regarding the roaster, after removing the bird, discard the fat and deglaze the pan with dry sherry. Combine with giblet broth to make gravy or store for later use.
